docs.title: Example Architecture Overview
docs.summary: A safe public example showing how to document a lab architecture without exposing private details.
docs.body:
Example Architecture Overview

This is demo content for a public GitHub template. Replace every placeholder with private production details only in your private deployment.

Example Layout

  • Internet → Gateway → Core switch → Server VLAN
  • Documentation service runs on an internal container host.
  • Secrets are referenced by location only, never stored directly.
Privacy Reminder

Do not publish real public IPs, private IPs, hostnames, user names, domains, runbooks, screenshots, backups, exports, or secret locations from your environment.

docs.title: Example Security Patching Project
docs.summary: Public-safe example of a security project entry.
docs.body:
Example Security Patching Project

This project demonstrates how to track remediation work without publishing real CVE findings, asset names, IP addresses, client names, or incident details.

Example Workflow

  1. Identify affected package or container.
  2. Review release notes.
  3. Patch in a maintenance window.
  4. Validate health checks.
  5. Document rollback notes.

runbooks.title: Restart an Example Docker Service
runbooks.summary: Safe generic restart workflow for a Docker Compose service.
steps: Open a shell on the container host.
Change to the application directory, for example: cd /opt/example-service.
Validate the application health endpoint or web UI.
runbooks.notes: Replace placeholders with real service names only in private documentation.

runbooks.title: Create Backup Before Change
runbooks.summary: Template runbook for taking a backup before modifying a service.
steps: Record current app version and container status.
Create a timestamped archive of the app directory.
Export current configuration and data files.
runbooks.notes: Public template only. Do not include real backup paths or credentials.
